title: Grünbaum's Conjecture (Conjecture 2)
statement_text: If $G$ is a 2-connected cubic graph embedded in the torus and $G$ is not 3-edge-colorable, then $G$ contains two edges on the same face of the embedding, whose removal gives a planar graph in which the four endpoints of the removed edges lie on the boundaries of two faces.
context_text: Grünbaum posed this conjecture in 1968 alongside a more general question of whether any snark can be the dual of a triangulation of a surface. Although the general statement fails for genus 5 (Kochol), the torus case is the central subject of this paper. The conjecture also motivated a further generalization by Thompson concerning $d$-edge-colorability of duals of triangulations of $(d-1)$-dimensional manifolds.
paper_contribution: Proves a strengthening: the only snarks embeddable in the torus are the Petersen graph and its dot products (the Blanuša-$H_k$ ($k\geq 1$) and Blanuša-$V_k$ ($k\geq 1$) families), implying that every dual of a triangulation of the torus has a Grünbaum coloring (proper 3-edge-coloring).
paper_title: Three-edge-coloring (Tait coloring) cubic graphs on the torus: A proof of Grünbaum's conjecture

title: Tutte 4-Flow Conjecture (Conjecture 1)
statement_text: Every 2-connected graph without a Petersen graph minor admits a nowhere-zero 4-flow.
context_text: Tutte's 4-Flow Conjecture remains open in general; its special case for cubic graphs was proved by Robertson, Seymour, and Thomas in 1997. The paper's main structural theorem implies a very strong version for toroidal graphs, well beyond what the conjecture requires on that surface.
paper_contribution: Proves that every 2-edge-connected graph embedded in the torus admits a nowhere-zero 4-flow unless it is Petersen-like (in which case it provably does not); this vastly strengthens the Tutte 4-Flow Conjecture on the torus because almost all toroidal graphs contain a Petersen minor but almost none are Petersen-like.
paper_title: Three-edge-coloring (Tait coloring) cubic graphs on the torus: A proof of Grünbaum's conjecture

title: Robertson's 4-Flow Conjecture for the Torus (Conjecture 3)
statement_text: Every 2-edge-connected graph in the torus whose representativity is at least 3, has a nowhere-zero 4-flow.
context_text: Robertson posed this conjecture in the mid-1990s (recorded as Problem 5.5.19 in [24]); it is a surface-specific instance of his more general Conjecture 4. The paper's Theorem 1.4 implies this conjecture.
paper_contribution: Proved as a corollary of Theorem 1.4, which establishes nowhere-zero 4-flows for all 2-edge-connected toroidal graphs that are not Petersen-like.
paper_title: Three-edge-coloring (Tait coloring) cubic graphs on the torus: A proof of Grünbaum's conjecture

title: Robertson's 4-Flow Conjecture for General Surfaces (Conjecture 4)
statement_text: For every surface $S$, there is a constant $r$ such that every 2-edge-connected graph embedded in $S$ with representativity at least $r$ has a nowhere-zero 4-flow.
context_text: Robertson made this conjecture in the mid-1990s as a surface-theoretic analogue of Grünbaum's Conjecture. It remains open for surfaces beyond the projective plane and the torus; the paper settles the torus case with $r=3$.
paper_contribution: Establishes the conjecture for the torus with constant $r=3$ via Theorem 1.4; the projective-plane case ($r=4$) was resolved by earlier work [16].
paper_title: Three-edge-coloring (Tait coloring) cubic graphs on the torus: A proof of Grünbaum's conjecture
